html:
THE VENDETTA GAZETTE

Special Edition • March 22, 2026 • "All the Blood That's Fit to Print"

IRON TITANS CLASH AGAIN AS WU-TANG AND SIR CAPRICE CONTINUE THEIR BLOODY WALTZ

The streets of Vendetta City trembled once more this week as the two undisputed sultans of sin, Wu-Tang "The Iron Fist of the East Side" and the immaculately dressed Sir Caprice, continued their deadly dance for dominance. In a repeat performance that had the entire underworld holding its collective breath, Wu-Tang seized 103 fresh turfs while Sir Caprice claimed 102 territories in what can only be described as the most gentlemanly rivalry this side of hell.

The Oriental mastermind Wu-Tang unleashed his fury with 125 separate raids, proving that last week's reign of terror was merely an appetizer. Not to be outdone, the dapper Sir Caprice conducted 121 strategic strikes with the precision of a Swiss timepiece and the brutality of a Chicago Valentine's Day.

Meanwhile, the vacation-loving duo of Vacation Steve and Vacation Albert proved that relaxation and racketeering make perfect bedfellows, each claiming 42 turfs while presumably still nursing their tropical tans. Sources suggest these brothers in both blood and leisure have formed what insiders are calling "The Cabana Cartel."

Blood Captain, that crimson-coated commander of chaos, stormed his way to 28 new territories and executed a spectacular 68 raids throughout the week. His biggest score came courtesy of poor Asiong Salonga, whose coffers were relieved of $500 in cold cash and 500 pieces of artillery in what witnesses describe as "the smoothest stick-up since Dillinger's heyday."

SPECIAL BULLETIN
HQ HOLOCAUST CONTINUES!

The week's most spine-tingling moment came when Wu-Tang's iron-fisted enforcers kicked down the doors of SPEROD's headquarters for the second time, proving that some lessons require repeated instruction! But the Oriental overlord wasn't finished with his educational program – his boys also schooled Aharhibaba, 2345p, and Muna in the finer points of territory management. Sir Caprice, ever the gentleman scholar, contributed his own lessons by storming the headquarters of Jeeva1661, jan kooh, Reti, Joshie, and ayan. A staggering 31 headquarters fell this week, with Wu-Tang claiming 17 scalps and Sir Caprice adding 13 more trophies to his ever-growing collection!

FALLEN EMPERORS AND TUMBLING KINGS

The mighty continue their spectacular falls from grace, dear readers! The FIFTH BLOOD EMPEROR – that once-fearsome sovereign of the streets – hemorrhaged another 28 turfs this week, bringing his two-week losing streak to a catastrophic 51 territories. His royal treasury was also ransacked 29 separate times, suggesting his imperial guard might want to consider new employment opportunities.

StreetKing, whose crown has been slipping faster than a banana peel on wet pavement, lost another 27 turfs while enduring 19 brutal raids. Combined with last week's losses, this former monarch of mayhem has surrendered over 50 territories in a fortnight – a fall from grace that would make Caesar weep.

FRESH BLOOD AND VANISHING ACTS

Six new desperados emerged from the shadows this week, including returning favorites Flash Gordon and John James Rambo 🩸, each claiming their inaugural turf with the determination of men who've seen too much action. Joining them are the mysterious Nima, Floyd, Prosdocimus, and OlegX7 – each carving out their first slice of Vendetta City's bloody pie.

However, 41 souls vanished from our criminal census this week, including poor Lusitanio, whose 4-turf empire crumbled before disappearing entirely. Whether they've assumed ambient temperature or simply caught the next train out of town remains a mystery suitable for our crime writers to ponder.

POLICE BLOTTER

• Officer O'Malley reports that Donald Trump conducted 28 raids this week, though witnesses claim he spent more time arguing about the quality of his opponents' headquarters than actually robbing them.

• The mysterious player known only as "" was raided 209 times this week, leading detectives to wonder if this phantom criminal actually exists or if he's just very, very unlucky.

• GodFather quietly claimed 15 turfs while everyone was watching the Wu-Tang and Sir Caprice show, proving that sometimes the best crime is the kind nobody notices.
